channel.title: Serverless on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Serverless on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Front Door edge actions
item.description:
Azure Front Door edge actions are now in public preview. They let customers run lightweight JavaScript during request processing at the edge for real-time decisions closer to users.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: See your serverless: introducing the Headlamp plugin for Knative
item.description:
Headlamp now has a Knative plugin that adds integrated views and actions for Knative resources in the Kubernetes UI. It centralizes KService management, traffic splitting, autoscaling configuration, metrics, and related CRDs, with release 0.3.0-beta available.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Go language support on Azure Functions
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports Go as a first-class language option in public preview. Developers can build event-driven serverless apps in Go using the Azure Functions Go programming model and SDK.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Serverless agents runtime for Azure Functions
item.description:
Azure Functions adds a public preview for serverless agents runtime, positioning AI agents as a first-class workload. The model is markdown-first and event-driven, allowing agents to be authored in natural language.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Serverless indexers in Azure AI Search
item.description:
Azure AI Search now has a public preview of serverless native indexers for ingestion workflows. This removes the need to provision or manage indexer compute, and indexer execution scales with workload demand.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Foundry IQ: Build smarter agents faster with unified knowledge and serverless retrieval
item.description:
Foundry IQ is presented as a way to ground agents in unified enterprise knowledge using serverless retrieval. The post emphasizes improved agentic retrieval quality and production-ready security.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions Support for Node.js 24
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports Node.js 24 in general availability. Apps can be developed locally with Node.js 24 and deployed to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including Flex Consumption.

  • Source: Azure Updates
